_note: Curated, illustrative summaries of well-known government agricultural support schemes (India-focused). For a real deployment these would be refreshed from official portals. Always verify on the official site before acting.

schemes.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
schemes.benefit: Income support of ₹6,000/year paid in three equal installments directly to bank accounts.
schemes.who: Landholding farmer families (subject to exclusion criteria).
schemes.how: Register at the PM-KISAN portal or via your local Common Service Centre with Aadhaar, land records, and bank details.

schemes.name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
schemes.benefit: Crop insurance against yield loss from drought, flood, pests, and disease at low farmer premiums (2% Kharif, 1.5% Rabi).
schemes.who: Farmers growing notified crops, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers.
schemes.how: Enroll through banks, insurance intermediaries, or the national crop insurance portal before the cut-off date for the season.

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.benefit: Free soil testing and a card with crop-wise nutrient and fertilizer recommendations every 2-3 years.
schemes.how: Request through your local agriculture office or soil testing lab; samples are collected and analysed.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.benefit: Short-term crop loans at subsidised interest (effective ~4% with prompt repayment), plus working capital for allied activities.
schemes.how: Apply at any bank branch with identity, address, and land documents.

schemes.name: PKVY (Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana)
schemes.benefit: Financial assistance for adopting organic farming in clusters, including certification and marketing support.
schemes.who: Groups of farmers forming organic clusters.
schemes.how: Join or form a cluster via the state agriculture department.

schemes.name: PMKSY (Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana)
schemes.benefit: Subsidies for micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler) to improve water-use efficiency: 'more crop per drop'.
schemes.how: Apply via the state horticulture/agriculture department or the micro-irrigation portal.

schemes.name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
schemes.benefit: Online trading platform connecting mandis so farmers get transparent, competitive prices for produce.
schemes.who: Farmers and traders registered at participating mandis.
schemes.how: Register at a participating mandi or on the e-NAM portal.
